title: From Lead Belly to Tupac: a prison history of music
description: In his book 'The Midnight Special', author Colin Asher traces how prisons and the criminal justice system shaped American music, from Lead Belly and Johnny Cash to Tupac.
hook: Prison shaped American music from Lead Belly to Tupac—a history told through the voices the system tried to silence.

title: There Are Further Zones
description: The scar and its synthetic monofilament sutures served as the blueprint for an impossible metropolis, its keloid ridges rising into serrated spires that defied human geometry. Under his fingertips, the skin pulsed with a cold, metallic autonomy, suggesting a labyrinthine city designed by a malign intelligence – or a hydrocephalic child – to house the anxieties colonising his adhesiolytic abdomen. He sensed the surgery as a structural intrusion – the implantation of a sightless, erotic citadel wi
hook: A scar becomes a city, skin pulses with metallic geometry—impossible, unsettling, and somehow deeply human.

title: Double Features: The Whole World’s On Fire
description: The Last of the Mohicans (1992) and The Insider (1999) bookend the history of the United States from Michael Mann’s perspective. Though their circumstances are worlds apart, their stories expose the history of America.
hook: Michael Mann's two films expose America's foundational violence, separated by centuries but telling the same story.

title: A Poet for Everyone, Even Those Who Don’t Read Poetry
description: The film “Mary Oliver: Saved by the Beauty of the World” works best when it illuminates her work, whose fans include Stephen Colbert and Oprah Winfrey.
hook: Mary Oliver's poetry reached Oprah and Colbert—a documentary on how one woman's sentences became everyone's permission slip.

title: How AI is changing language
description:
As allegations of LLM use rock the literary and media worlds, linguists explain what really distinguishes human and machine writing, while novelists including Jennifer Egan and Jeanette Winterson reflect on the future of fiction in an age of ChatGPT

Three paragraphs, from three different hotel reviews. Can you tell which, if any, were AI‑generated?

“The hotel is in a great location for everything. Lots of places to eat and drink. The hotel itself is always abuzz.
hook: What actually separates human writing from AI? Novelists like Jennifer Egan confront the uncanny mirror ChatGPT holds up.

title: Akira Ikezoe’s Frogs and Bears Have Something Urgent to Tell Us
description: The cartoonish earnestness of both artist and artwork belies a sharp attentiveness to the catastrophes unfolding around us.
hook: Akira Ikezoe's cartoon frogs and bears deliver urgent warnings wrapped in disarming cuteness.

title: Soccer Has Arrived: How the 2026 World Cup Blew Past Every TV Ratings Forecast
description: The 2026 FIFA World Cup has smashed TV ratings records on Fox and Telemundo, with 43M+ viewers tuning in and a potential 50M for a USMNT final.
hook: The 2026 World Cup shattered every ratings prediction—soccer finally arrived in America's living rooms.
